key_departures.impact: Five-year Colt and former first-round pick departed after a down 2025 (4 sacks in 17 games). Signed 3yr/$48M ($32M gtd) with Las Vegas. Despite his decline, he was a starter and opens a pass-rush hole opposite Laiatu Latu.

Signed a 1-year deal (terms undisclosed) with Atlanta. Had career-high 9.5 sacks in 2023 but missed all of 2024 (torn Achilles) and was limited to just 2 sacks in 2025. Departure removes a veteran pass-rush presence.

8-year starting right tackle (105 starts) signed 2yr/$25M ($13.5M gtd) with Houston. Departure creates a starting RT vacancy. Colts will turn to 2025 4th-round pick Jalen Travis, who started four games late in 2025 with solid PFF grades.
Signed 2yr/$14M with Washington. A 38-start strong safety who developed into one of the better box safeties in the AFC. Departure creates a starting safety hole alongside newly signed Camryn Bynum.
Depth safety signed with Seattle. 26 starts over 4 years with the Colts. Depth loss at a position already thin after Cross's departure.
Signed 2yr/$12M with Chicago. One-year Colt who posted a career-high 3.5 sacks in 2025. Depth/rotational loss behind Buckner and Stewart.

8-year Colt traded to Green Bay for DT Colby Wooden. Pro Bowl-caliber LB who was the defensive leader. Trade saves $5.75M in cap space but leaves a massive hole at LB — Jaylon Carlies (limited 2025 action) now projects as the top LB.

Traded to Pittsburgh for a 2026 6th-round pick (received a 7th in return). Colts received a 6th-rounder. Created WR depth need alongside Pierce and Josh Downs, though Pierce steps into a larger WR1 role.

signings.analysis: The richest 2-year deal in NFL history. Jones gets $50M guaranteed in Year 1 alone, giving the Colts a committed 2-year window. He threw for 3,101 yards, 19 TDs, 8 INTs, 68% completion rate in 2025 before a torn Achilles. At $44M AAV he's mid-tier for elite QB contracts but fully starter-committed. The 2-year structure means the QB need will re-emerge in 2028, but for this draft cycle the position is fully addressed. Health in 2026 is the one caveat — Jones is recovering from an Achilles tear but the Colts believe he'll be ready Week 1.

Four-year, $114M max deal with $60M fully guaranteed at signing and $84M total guarantees — the most guaranteed money ever for a free-agent WR. Pierce led the NFL in yards per catch (21.3) for the second consecutive year and eclipsed 1,000 receiving yards for the first time. Now the unquestioned WR1 after Pittman's departure. The 4-year commitment with massive guarantees signals the Colts view him as a long-term cornerstone. WR1 need is fully addressed for the foreseeable future; a complementary WR2/slot upgrade remains desirable given Pittman's departure.

Three-year/$60M deal with $35M guaranteed and $20M signing bonus. Ward's 41 pass deflections since joining SF in 2022 rank top-5 in the NFL. He steps in as the CB2 opposite Sauce Gardner (acquired via trade deadline). At $20M AAV, this is solid starter-tier value. Combined with Gardner's $120.4M extension the Colts have invested heavily at CB — the position moves from a need to a strength.

Four-year/$60M with $26M guaranteed and $18M signing bonus. Bynum (one of four players with 300+ tackles and 7+ INTs since 2023) fills the free safety role. However, Nick Cross's departure at strong safety leaves the other starting spot unresolved — Juanyeh Thomas (36 career games) is the current placeholder at SS. Bynum addresses one safety spot fully; the strong safety/box safety role created by Cross's exit is still a concern. S remains a partial need.

Acquired at the 2025 trade deadline for the 2026 (No. 15 overall) and 2027 first-round picks plus WR Adonai Mitchell. Gardner's 4-year/$120.4M extension (signed with NYJ) transferred with him — the highest-paid CB in NFL history. Two-time first-team All-Pro in his first two seasons. Projects as CB1 alongside Ward and Bynum in a revamped secondary. The trade cost the Colts their top two first-round picks (no 2026 or 2027 first-rounders). CB is a position of strength, not need.

Two-year/$20M (up to) with $11M guaranteed. Key is a career rotational rusher with 30.5 career sacks across 8 NFL seasons. He fills a rotation role but ESPN explicitly noted his signing 'won't preclude the Colts from signing or drafting additional edge players.' At $10M AAV he is below starter tier for EDGE ($15M+). The Colts lost Paye, Ebukam, and Lewis — Key alone doesn't solve the EDGE problem. EDGE remains a significant draft need.

Three-year/$17.5M (max $18.5M). Clemons had 8.5 sacks in 4 seasons with the Jets, totaling 20 QB hits and 13 TFLs. Projects as a rotational piece. At $5.8M AAV this is depth-level money. Combined with Key, the Colts have added two rotational EDGE players but no clear starter. EDGE pass-rush remains an unaddressed primary need for the draft.

signings.previous_team: Green Bay Packers (trade for Zaire Franklin)
signings.analysis: Acquired in the Franklin trade. The 25-year-old started 16 games for Green Bay in 2025. He's a young developmental IDL piece behind Buckner and Stewart. Contract details not yet disclosed (was on rookie deal). IDL depth is adequately addressed with Buckner, Stewart, Wooden, Nnadi, and Adebawore.

3-time Super Bowl champion with 130 career games and 98 starts. Provides veteran IDL depth behind Buckner and Stewart. Contract terms not disclosed. This is a veteran minimum/low-cost depth add. IDL depth is now well-stocked with Buckner, Stewart, Wooden, Nnadi, and Adebawore — the position doesn't need a draft pick.

One-year deal at near-minimum ($1.34M). Herbert has averaged 4.8 yards per carry over his NFL career. He provides a quality backup option behind Jonathan Taylor. Low-cost move that adequately fills the backup RB role. RB depth need minimally addressed — it's unlikely a premium draft pick is needed here.
Re-signed on a low-cost deal. Ogletree earned the top pass-blocking grade among all TEs in 2025 per PFF. He's a blocking specialist behind Tyler Warren. TE is not a need — Warren is a young starter.
Re-signed on a low-cost deal. Has not caught a pass as a Colt but saw 51% of special teams snaps in 2025. This is a camp/special teams body. WR depth/WR2 need remains active.
Re-signed after being an undrafted FA presence since 2019. A core special teamer. Does not address the WR2/slot need created by Pittman's departure.
Re-signed after going 11/11 on FGs (including a franchise-record 60-yarder) in 5 games in 2025. Kicking competition with recovering Spencer Shrader expected. Not a draft need.
Retained via exclusive rights tag at $1.005M. Had 32 tackles in 16 games in 2025 as a depth piece. Does not address the critical LB void left by Zaire Franklin's trade. LB remains a top draft need.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Daniel Jones re-signed on a 2yr/$88M deal ($60M gtd, $50M in Year 1 alone). He's the unquestioned starter for 2026-2027. The position is fully resolved for this draft cycle. Note: the 2-year structure means QB returns as a need in 2028 draft cycle, but it's off the board entirely in 2026.

Sauce Gardner (4yr/$120.4M, acquired via trade deadline) plus Charvarius Ward (3yr/$60M, $35M gtd) plus re-installed Kenny Moore II at nickel create arguably the best CB room in the AFC. The Colts gave up two first-round picks (2026 + 2027) to acquire Gardner — they are fully committed. CB is a strength, not a need.

Alec Pierce re-signed (4yr/$114M, $60M fully guaranteed) and is now the unquestioned WR1. However, Michael Pittman Jr. was traded to Pittsburgh, and the WR2/slot role is thin — Josh Downs is the primary slot option with Ashton Dulin and Laquon Treadwell as depth only. A complementary WR2 (outside or slot) is a legitimate rounds 2-4 draft target, especially with the Colts lacking a first-round pick.

Kwity Paye (to Raiders, 3yr/$48M), Samson Ebukam (to Falcons, 1yr), and Tyquan Lewis (departed) all left. Replacements are Arden Key (2yr/$20M, rotational) and Micheal Clemons (3yr/$17.5M, rotational). Neither is a proven starter. Laiatu Latu is the only true starter-caliber edge rusher. EDGE is now the #1 draft priority given the total depth problem. The Colts don't have a 1st-round pick, making a high-value Day 2 EDGE a must.

Zaire Franklin was traded to Green Bay (for Colby Wooden). Franklin was an 8-year starter, Pro Bowler, and defensive signal-caller. Jaylon Carlies 'hardly touched the field in 2025' and is now the nominal LB1. Austin Ajiake is depth only. No quality free-agent LB was signed. LB is co-equal with EDGE as the most critical draft need — the Colts need a starter-caliber LB in rounds 2-3.

Braden Smith (8-year starter) departed to Houston (2yr/$25M). The replacement plan is Jalen Travis, a 2025 4th-round pick with 4 starts and a solid 74.2 PFF grade. Travis is promising but unproven over a full season. Bernhard Raimann continues at LT. OT is escalated — a developmental OT or veteran backup in rounds 3-5 would provide insurance behind Travis.

Camryn Bynum (4yr/$60M) fills the free safety role credibly. However, Nick Cross's departure (2yr/$14M to Washington) leaves strong safety as a genuine hole — Juanyeh Thomas has 36 career games and 4 INTs, but is unproven as a starter. Rodney Thomas II also departed to Seattle. The FS spot is addressed but SS/box safety is a live need. S remains tier 2 — a Day 2 safety would be a quality addition.

Despite losing Neville Gallimore (to Bears, 2yr/$12M), the Colts added Colby Wooden (via trade, started 16 games for GB in 2025), Derrick Nnadi (veteran depth), and retain DeForest Buckner and Grover Stewart. IDL depth is well-stocked. Not a draft priority.

Split into WR1 (fully addressed by Pierce re-signing) vs WR2/depth (escalated by Pittman departure). Net assessment is tier 2 — a developmental or starter-caliber complement to Pierce is needed to support the offense. Josh Downs profiles as the slot/WR2, but an outside complement and overall depth upgrade is warranted.

key_additions.headline: Richest 2-year QB deal in NFL history locks in Colts' starter after career year (3,101 yds, 68% completion rate)
Record-setting WR extension ($29M AAV, most-ever for free-agent WR) makes Pierce the Colts' WR1 for the next four years
Elite CB2 with 41 PDs since 2022 joins Sauce Gardner to give Colts arguably the best cornerback tandem in the AFC
Ballard's biggest free-agent investment ever in a safety — Bynum brings elite tackling and ball-hawking instincts to the Colts' rebuilt secondary
Two-time All-Pro and highest-paid CB in NFL history gives Indy a shutdown No. 1 corner acquired at the trade deadline
Veteran pass-rush addition (30.5 career sacks) provides EDGE rotation depth alongside Laiatu Latu
Young EDGE piece (8.5 sacks in 4 Jets seasons) adds pass-rush depth on a team-friendly deal

key_additions.contract_summary: Acquired via trade (Zaire Franklin to GB)
key_additions.headline: 25-year-old IDL who started 16 games for the Packers in 2025 arrives as the headline return in the Franklin trade
3-time Super Bowl champion and 98-game starter bolsters IDL depth behind Buckner and Stewart
Veteran backup (4.8 career YPC) gives Colts reliable insurance behind Jonathan Taylor at near-minimum cost

key_losses.headline: 8-year Colt and former WR1 traded to Pittsburgh for a 6th-round pick, freeing cap and clearing the path for Pierce's record extension
Pro Bowl LB and 8-year defensive leader traded away, leaving the Colts without a proven starter at linebacker
Former first-round pick and 5-year starter departs in free agency, leaving a significant hole in the Colts' pass rush
8-year starting right tackle (105 starts) departs for a division rival, handing the starting RT job to unproven 2025 4th-rounder Jalen Travis
Ascending strong safety and 38-game starter leaves, opening a hole alongside newly signed Camryn Bynum
Pass-rush veteran (9.5 sacks in 2023 with Colts) departs after slower-than-hoped Achilles recovery in 2025
